general_hypothesis: Within a fixed wall-clock budget, throughput and a real anneal schedule matter more than any single hyperparameter, so mixed-precision + larger batch + cosine anneal + weight EMA should dominate the naive baseline.
detailed_hypothesis: bf16 AMP raises throughput (more steps/hr), batch 32 stabilises the gradient, a warmup-cosine schedule gives a genuine late-training anneal, and a weight EMA (decay 0.995) averages away late-training noise on the honest metrics.
experiment_note: bf16 AMP + bs32 + lr4e-4 + warmup-cosine + EMA0.995 + wd1e-4 (tuned across E0b/E0c/E14/E16/E17/E19/E21/E79/E82/E91).
conclusion: PASS. The optimisation envelope is fully mapped: lr4e-4/EMA0.995/wd1e-4/1-epoch-warmup/bs32 are each a mapped optimum; off-optimum settings U-turn. This is the foundation every later study builds on.

general_hypothesis: Layout rays that only read audio independently cannot enforce a globally consistent scene; letting the coarse rays exchange information should improve global layout.
detailed_hypothesis: A ray<->ray global self-attention over the 16x32 coarse ray grid (512 tokens) lets layout rays reason jointly after reading audio, before decoding to finer scales.
experiment_note: Add one global self-attention block on the 16x32 coarse grid.
conclusion: PASS. Coarse ray<->ray self-attention was the first architectural win (E22). Capacity saturates immediately: depth (E23), heads (E25), mid-scale/global (E24/E26) add nothing or bust budget.

general_hypothesis: Ray-pair interactions on a sphere are governed by angular geometry, so making the coarse self-attention geometry-aware should help more than plain attention.
detailed_hypothesis: Add a learned per-head bias on the cos angular distance between every ray pair to the coarse self-attention logits (geometry-biased attention).
experiment_note: Add cos-angular-distance bias MLP to the coarse self-attention.
conclusion: PASS. Geometry bias helped (E27). Enriching with absolute elevation (E28) biased toward the gamed ABS_REL and lost the honest metrics -> keep the minimal geometric feature at this stage.

general_hypothesis: Global spatial reasoning may be most effective AFTER local acoustic evidence and encoder detail have been fused into a coherent coarse scene, not on raw ray tokens.
detailed_hypothesis: Apply stacked geometry-aware ray self-attention (rsa16b) to the FUSED coarse representation m16 (post encoder-skip), with progressively richer geometric features (cos-dist, elevation, wrapped azimuth).
experiment_note: Two geometry-aware self-attn blocks on fused m16, geom feats = [cosd, elev_i, elev_j, cos(daz), sin(daz)].
conclusion: PASS but PARTLY NOISE. Post-fusion geometry reopened the frontier (E50/E51 real). Later micro-wins (E56/E57) were within noise: the E60 confirmation rerun of E57 came out 0.027 WORSE, revealing true sigma~0.019 on a single pair and that recent sub-0.015 gains were not distinguishable. Depth saturates at 2 blocks.

general_hypothesis: The decoder should admit encoder detail selectively per scale rather than adding all skip features uniformly.
detailed_hypothesis: Gate the DPT encoder skips (g4/g3): the ray features decide via a sigmoid gate how much encoder detail to admit at each scale.
experiment_note: Gated DPT skips at 16x32 and 32x64.
conclusion: PASS. Gated skips helped (E29). Dropping the finest F64 cross-attn (E65) was a major efficiency win (549->380s/ep, VRAM 30->17.5GB, more anneal epochs). FiLM audio modulation (E44/E63) and learned full-decode (E64/E66) are neutral or budget-busting -> resolution/audio-modulation not the bottleneck.

general_hypothesis: The dense MAE alone under-weights the metrics that matter; auxiliary loss terms aligned with the eval metrics may help, but any term that directly optimises an honest metric will game it.
detailed_hypothesis: Add a relative-error term (targets ABS_REL), a coarse-layout term, a low-pass smoothness term, and a light edge/gradient term; test SILog, berHu, log-depth, Charbonnier, per-sample normalisation.
experiment_note: Composite = MAE + 0.1*rel + 1.0*coarse + 0.5*low + 0.05*grad; sweep alternatives.
conclusion: PASS(partial). rel(w=0.1) + light grad(0.05) + load-bearing coarse/low aux terms are the tuned objective. berHu/SILog/log-depth/Charbonnier/per-sample-norm are FRONTIER SLIDES (trade ABS_REL<->RMSE within noise). Dropping aux losses (E62) costs 0.070 -> coarse+low are load-bearing for the honest metrics.

general_hypothesis: The binaural spatial cues fed to the network may be improvable, but the STFT+cue construction lives in the fixed prepare.py so only coarse choices (in_ch, augmentation, positional info) are currently testable.
detailed_hypothesis: Test in_ch in {2,3,5}, drop IPD phase feats, add CoordConv/positional embeddings/SH/mic PE, and input level-jitter augmentation.
experiment_note: Vary in_ch flag; add coord/PE features; level-jitter aug.
conclusion: FAIL (informative). 5ch [logL,logR,ILD,cosIPD,sinIPD] is optimal: dropping IPD phase costs 0.055 (phase encodes direction), absolute level is a LOAD-BEARING depth cue (jitter costs 0.081), and explicit positional info (CoordConv/SH/mic/learned PE) is neutral-to-harmful (conv encoder captures position). Representation search is gated by prepare.py -> see PROPOSAL-01.

general_hypothesis: If the model were capacity- or resolution-limited, widening/deepening or higher-res decode would help.
detailed_hypothesis: Widen dim 192->256, deepen attention, learned high-res decode, bottleneck refine, encoder positional embeddings.
experiment_note: dim/heads/decoder-resolution/encoder sweeps.
conclusion: FAIL. The model is NOT capacity- or resolution-limited (E68 wider worse; E66 higher-res worse). It is anneal-limited and ultimately audio->depth-information-limited. Heavy capacity busts the 1-hour budget -> fewer epochs -> auto-loses.

general_hypothesis: Removing provably-dead or redundant modules should be free or better, and simpler is preferred on ties.
detailed_hypothesis: Drop the dead g2 gate, the vestigial F64 modules, the SwiGLU class, and the vestigial pix2pix encoder tail e5-e8 (never reached by forward).
experiment_note: Remove dead code; verify output-equivalence modulo init-RNG.
conclusion: PASS. E116 dropped the pix2pix encoder tail (24.47->7.69M params, -69%) provably output-equivalent. Pure simplification win; became the pre-TTA base champion fef2779.

general_hypothesis: The geometry-aware reasoning that helped ray<->ray self-attention might also help the ray<->audio CROSS-attention, since at the coarse scale the ray queries and the e4 audio tokens share the same 16x32 ERP grid.
detailed_hypothesis: A GeoCrossBlock adds a learned angular-distance bias between the ray-query direction and the audio-token direction so each coarse ray reads the geometrically-relevant audio tokens (cr16); tested deeper (E124) and at the 32x64 scale (E123).
experiment_note: Replace cr16 CrossBlock with a geometry-biased GeoCrossBlock.
conclusion: FAIL (critical, informative). E121/E122 looked like a 0.010 win on two low draws and was crowned, but the E125 third draw (2.1132) pulled the 3-draw mean to 2.097 == old baseline 2.099. Geometry-cross-attn is WITHIN NOISE. Extending to 32x64 busts budget (E123, 784s/ep); deeper saturates (E124). LESSON (repeat of E60): never crown a sub-0.015 candidate on <3 confirming draws.

general_hypothesis: A small learned prior codebook the coarse rays can attend to might store common room-layout priors and aid generalisation to unseen scenes.
detailed_hypothesis: Append K=8 learnable register/summary KV tokens to the coarse cross-attention so rays can read a learned scratchpad, distinct from audio-derived global modulation.
experiment_note: Append 8 learnable KV tokens to cr16 audio KV.
conclusion: FAIL. Neutral (within-noise champion-range draw). Consistent with global-audio modulation (E44/E63) being mapped: the cross-attention already supplies sufficient conditioning.

general_hypothesis: The one exact physical symmetry of binaural-audio-in-a-room is the left/right mirror; the model is trained to respect it (flip-aug), so ensembling the two symmetric views at inference should cut prediction variance and improve all metrics at once (not a frontier slide).
detailed_hypothesis: At eval, average the prediction with the mirrored-input prediction (swap audio L/R, flip depth-width back). Deterministic honest ensembling over the L/R symmetry; prepare.py/compute_errors untouched.
experiment_note: Add L/R-flip TTA to evaluate(): D = 0.5*(model(x) + flip(model(swap_lr(x)))).
conclusion: PASS -> GLOBAL CHAMPION. 3 draws {2.0720,2.0782,2.0872} mean 2.079+-0.008, ~2.6 sigma below old champion 2.099; RMSE 1.464 & d1 0.583 best-ever, ABS_REL improved too. Deterministic (not a noise gamble). Azimuth-rotation TTA is INVALID (audio cues tied to fixed mic orientation) so 2-way flip is the TTA ceiling.

general_hypothesis: If per-ray depth were multi-modal, a distributional (bin-classification) decode could beat direct regression.
detailed_hypothesis: Replace the sigmoid depth head with an AdaBins-style soft-argmax over 64 fixed depth bins (softmax -> expectation).
experiment_note: Head outputs 64 bin logits; depth = softmax . bin_centers.
conclusion: FAIL. +0.042 worse; d1 regressed hard (0.568 vs 0.582). The audio->depth field is smooth (S05/E66), so soft-argmax expectation-smoothing blurs it; bins also train slower within the budget. The decode-paradigm is not the bottleneck.

general_hypothesis: If TTA helps because the base model is only approximately L/R-equivariant, then explicitly training for equivariance could improve the base predictions (better held-out-scene generalisation) and tighten the TTA ensemble.
detailed_hypothesis: A stop-gradient teacher forward on the L/R-flipped input; penalise disagreement between the model's prediction and its own mirrored prediction (self-distillation for equivariance).
experiment_note: Add loss += 0.1*|D - flip(model(swap_lr(x)).detach())|; extra no_grad forward per step (E131 full-batch, E132 quarter-batch).
conclusion: FAIL. Structural screen E131 (full-batch teacher) BUST the budget at 552s/ep (~7 epochs); refined E132 (quarter-batch teacher, 421s/ep, 8 epochs) scored comp 2.0925 vs TTA champion 2.079 -> +0.013 WORSE. ABS_REL improved (0.338) but RMSE/d1 regressed -> frontier slide. FAILURE MODE: training-time L/R-equivariance is REDUNDANT with the free eval-time TTA (S12) that already captures the symmetry at zero training cost, while the extra teacher forward gives up ~1 anneal epoch. Lesson: do not spend training compute to reproduce a symmetry that TTA already ensembles for free.

general_hypothesis: After a run of FAILed exploratory studies (S10 geometry-cross-attn, S13 bins, S14 equivariance) the architecture frontier looks saturated, so the TTA champion mean deserves a 4th confirming draw before being treated as the settled final method.
detailed_hypothesis: Repeat the exact TTA champion config; a consistent draw in the 2.07-2.09 band strengthens the ~2.08 mean.
experiment_note: Re-run champion 494b5e2 unchanged (E133).
conclusion: PASS (confirm). E133=2.0935; TTA 4-draw mean 2.0827+-0.008, ~2 sigma below old baseline 2.099. Champion is the settled final method under the fixed budget+representation. Architecture axes are mapped; the next genuinely-new gain likely requires the acoustic-representation axis (PROPOSAL-01), gated behind a prepare.py refactor.

general_hypothesis: Eval-time L/R-flip TTA averages the two symmetric views uniformly (50/50), but a model error is spatially structured, so the two mirrored views may not be equally reliable per-pixel; a per-pixel reliability weighting could beat uniform averaging.
detailed_hypothesis: A lightweight per-pixel confidence head trained on the DETACHED depth predicts local log-error; at eval, blend the two flip views weighted by predicted precision exp(-conf) instead of 50/50.
experiment_note: E134: shared head trunk + 1-ch confidence head; aux loss regressing conf to |D.detach()-gt|; precision-weighted TTA blend in evaluate().
conclusion: FAIL. E134=2.0966 vs champion 4-draw mean 2.083 -> +0.014 worse (d1 regressed 0.5767 vs 0.582). FAILURE MODE: uniform 50/50 is near-optimal for two SYMMETRIC equally-good views (minimises expected MSE); learned per-pixel confidence is noisy on held-out scenes so non-uniform weighting ADDS variance instead of reducing it, plus adds a head+loss. Confirms the TTA blend is already optimal; no free lunch in the weighting.

general_hypothesis: Depth from echoes is audio->depth information-limited (S08); the fixed single full-window STFT mixes the direct/early-reflection structure (nearby distances) with the reverberant tail (room volume), so an explicit early-vs-late temporal decomposition may expose depth cues the mixed representation obscures.
detailed_hypothesis: Split the fixed echo waveform in time into early + late windows, compute the baseline 5ch spatial feature on each, feed both as 10ch.
experiment_note: E136: prepare.FEATURE_FN=early_late_feat (5ch on each half-window) -> 10ch; block-aware swap keeps flip-aug+TTA valid.
conclusion: FAIL. E136=2.1247 vs champion 2.082 -> +0.043 worse (below old baseline 2.099). FAILURE MODE: splitting the WAVEFORM truncates each block STFT (fewer time frames, less info/block) and the baseline single full-window STFT already spreads echo-timing across width W, so the temporal decomposition is redundant AND lossy. The temporal axis is not the representation bottleneck; the time-frequency RESOLUTION tradeoff (S19 multi-res STFT) is the better lever. NOTE: PROPOSAL-01 seam works cleanly (first real representation study executed).

general_hypothesis: The single-resolution STFT forces one time-vs-frequency tradeoff, but echo DELAYS (which directly encode distance) want fine TIME resolution while room modes/materials want fine FREQUENCY; a multi-resolution representation could give sharper echo-delay localisation than the fixed baseline can resolve.
detailed_hypothesis: Concatenate baseline 5ch (n_fft512, fine freq) with short-window 5ch (n_fft128, ~4x finer time) -> 10ch input.
experiment_note: E137: prepare.FEATURE_FN=multires_feat (ds._specN 512 + _feat5_at wav,128,40,128); in_ch=10; block-aware swap keeps flip-aug+TTA valid.
conclusion: PASS -> NEW GLOBAL CHAMPION. 3-draw mean 2.0639 +-0.004 (tight), ALL below old TTA champion best draw 2.072; Delta -0.018 (~2.3sigma) vs old mean 2.082. Improves ABS_REL (0.327 vs 0.338) + honest d1 (0.588 vs 0.582) with RMSE ~tied (1.475 vs 1.465). Fine-TIME-resolution echo-delay features add REAL depth signal (distance ~ echo arrival time). FIRST gain beyond the architecture ceiling, via the PROPOSAL-01 representation axis. Confirmed E138/E139 (all 3 draws out of old-champion distribution -> not an E121-style edge draw).

general_hypothesis: The multi-res STFT win comes from the fine-time-resolution block that sharpens echo-delay/distance cues; the fine-block n_fft is the crux hyperparameter mapping the time-vs-frequency tradeoff.
detailed_hypothesis: Sweep the fine-block n_fft {64,128,256} vs the champion 128 to find the resolution that best localises echo delays.
experiment_note: E140 fine n_fft=64; E141 fine n_fft=256; champion=128.
conclusion: PASS(tune, no champion change). Fine-block sweep: 64=2.0652 (tied), 128=2.064 (best=champion), 256=2.0815 (+0.018 WORSE). n_fft=128 is the tuned optimum; the effect is fine-TIME resolution (64 finest ties, 256 coarser-time hurts by losing echo timing). Confirms the S19 mechanism (distance ~ echo arrival time). Champion unchanged (828b9a3).

general_hypothesis: The multi-res champion spans two time-frequency scales (512 fine-freq + 128 fine-time); room modes / wall materials leave fine SPECTRAL structure a longer window resolves better, so a third longest-window scale may add room/material cues.
detailed_hypothesis: Add a 3rd 5ch block at n_fft=1024 (finest frequency) on top of 512+128 -> 15ch.
conclusion: FAIL(redundant). E142=2.0615 is WITHIN the champion draw range (2.0595-2.0691) = tied, and 15ch is slower (only 8 epochs, under-anneals). The 3rd finer-FREQUENCY scale adds nothing -> confirms S20 that frequency is not the lever (time-resolution / echo-timing is). Simpler+faster 2-scale champion kept (828b9a3). Representation gains need a qualitatively-NEW cue, not more resolution.

general_hypothesis: More time-frequency RESOLUTION is exhausted (S20/S21); the remaining representation headroom is a qualitatively NEW cue. Interaural coherence separates direct/early sound (correlated wavefront) from diffuse late reverb (decorrelated) - a direct-vs-diffuse ratio encoding distance/room size differently from the instantaneous ILD/IPD magnitude+phase.
detailed_hypothesis: Add interaural magnitude-squared coherence per TF bin (smoothed ~3 frames over STFT time) as an L/R-symmetric channel appended to the 10ch multi-res champion.
experiment_note: E143: multires_coh_feat (10ch + coherence at n_fft=512 -> 11ch); generalized swap_lr_multi to pass trailing symmetric channels through the mirror unchanged.
conclusion: PASS -> NEW GLOBAL CHAMPION. 3-draw mean 2.0417 (all below multi-res-10ch best draw 2.0595; Delta -0.022 vs its mean 2.064). Improves the HONEST metrics (RMSE ~1.456 < 1.4645, d1 ~0.592 > 0.588), ABS_REL held -> not gaming. Coherence (direct-vs-diffuse) is a genuinely new cue adding real depth signal beyond ILD/IPD. 2nd representation-axis win. Commit 93ef41b, in_ch=11.

general_hypothesis: Coherence at the coarse 512 scale helped (S22); coherence is scale-dependent (the direct-vs-diffuse balance differs with analysis window), so adding coherence at the FINE 128 scale too may capture complementary direct/diffuse structure.
detailed_hypothesis: Append a 2nd coherence channel computed at n_fft=128 alongside the 512 coherence -> 12ch (10 multi-res + 2 coherence).
experiment_note: E146: multires_coh_feat appends _coherence_at(wav,128,...) as a 2nd trailing symmetric channel; in_ch=12.
conclusion: PASS -> NEW GLOBAL CHAMPION. 12ch 3-draw mean 2.0300 +-0.0018 (very tight), ALL below 11ch champion best draw 2.0320; Delta -0.012 (clears noise). Best-ever RMSE 1.452 + d1 0.597. Fine-scale (128) coherence adds complementary direct-vs-diffuse structure -> the two-scale coherence mirrors the two-scale multi-res design. Commit 36c6538, in_ch=12.

general_hypothesis: The coherence estimate uses a short temporal moving average; the smoothing window trades estimate stability vs time-localisation, so it may sharpen the coherence cue.
detailed_hypothesis: Sweep coherence smoothing taps (5 vs current 3) on both coherence channels.
experiment_note: E149 smooth=5; E150 confirm.
conclusion: NEUTRAL (no change). smooth=5 2-draw {2.0201,2.0389} mean 2.0295 =~ champion smooth=3 2.030; draws straddle the champion (E149 lucky-low, E150 high). Smoothing taps are not a lever. Keep smooth=3 champion 36c6538. Within-cue tuning (3rd res scale S21, smooth S24) now saturating -> a NEW cue is needed for further gains.
